items.description: The International Organization for Standardization two character representation for the given locale.
A three digit code associated with the given locale and used in conjunction with a Mobile Network Code to uniquely identify mobile networks.
The dialing prefix used to establish a call or send an SMS from one locale to the given locale.
Whether Twilio supports two-way SMS in the given locale.
Whether number portability is available in the given locale.

Concatenation refers to the capability of splitting a message that is too long to be sent in one SMS into smaller pieces and then joining the pieces at the receiving end so that the receiver sees the message as one.
How many characters can be sent given a particular message encoding before the message will be split into concatenated segments.
Multimedia Messaging Service (MMS) provides a standards-based means to send pictures and video to mobile phones.
How Twilio handles an SMS message destined for landline telephone number.
items.value: Twilio will not check whether the number is a landline and will attempt to send it to our carrier for delivery. Some carriers will convert the SMS into text-to-speech messages via voice calls.

items.description: Twilio strongly encourages customers to review proposed use cases with qualified legal counsel to make sure that they comply with all applicable laws. This table lists some general best practices.
items.value: Twilio’s customers, including their end users and clients, must comply with applicable laws, regulations, Twilio's policies, including, but not limited, to the Twilio Acceptable Use Policy and the Twilio Messaging Policy, and industry standards, including, but not limited to, telecommunications providers’ policies. U.S. telecommunications providers may assess fees for non-compliant A2P traffic, and Twilio will pass these fees onto you. To date, T-Mobile is the first U.S. telecommunications provider to announce non-compliance fees for violations of T-Mobile’s Code of Conduct. Twilio will update these guidelines accordingly if/when additional U.S. telecommunications providers announce non-compliance fees. T-Mobile non-compliance fees are as follows: 10DLC Long Code Messaging Program Evasion: A $1,000 pass-through fee if a program/campaign is found to be using techniques such as snowshoeing, or unauthorized number replacement/recycling. Content Violation: After prior warning, a $10,000 pass-through fee may be imposed for each unique instance of content violating the T-Mobile Code of Conduct involving the same sender/content provider. This includes SHAFT (Sex, Hate, Alcohol, Firearms, Tobacco) violations, spam, phishing, and messaging that meets the Severity 0 violation as defined in the CTIA Short Code Monitoring Handbook.

items.description: Whether mobile operators in the given country support the feature.
Whether Twilio supports the feature for the given country.

In some countries sender IDs for certain sender types are not preserved and are changed for compliance and/or deliverability reasons. In these countries mobile subscribers will see a different ‘from sender ID’ than the one sent by you.

Provisioning is the process of getting the sender ID approved and registered with mobile networks (depending on country requirements). Provisioning time is how long this process takes in the given country.

values.Pre-registration: Note: The euro symbol (€) is not supported; avoid using this character in message submission toward the United States.
values.Dynamic: Note: The euro symbol (€) is not supported; avoid using this character in message submission toward the United States.
